seoTitle: How to Add a Logo in Power BI Desktop to Brand Reports
metaDescription: Learn how to add a logo in Power BI Desktop so your reports reflect your company's branding consistently across dashboards and presentations.
intro: A report without any branding can feel generic, especially when it's going to stakeholders outside your immediate team. This guide covers how to add a logo in Power BI Desktop so your reports carry consistent brand identity. It's a simple step that helps dashboards look more polished and recognizable as they get shared around.

steps.heading: Navigate the Power BI interface
steps.body: Open the Insert ribbon in Power BI Desktop, where you'll find the Image option used to bring your logo file onto the report canvas.

steps.heading: Add your logo
steps.body: Browse to your logo file and insert it, placing it in a location that fits your layout, such as a top corner near the report title.

steps.heading: Resize your logo
steps.body: Adjust the logo's size so it complements the report without dominating the page, keeping it proportionate to other header elements.

steps.heading: Apply best practices for branding
steps.body: Keep the logo's placement and sizing consistent across every page and every report you build, so your organization's reports feel unified.

steps.heading: Consider professional use cases
steps.body: Use branded reports for client-facing presentations, sales dashboards, or any report leaving your internal team where brand consistency matters.

faq.question: What image format should I use for a Power BI logo?
faq.answer: A PNG file with a transparent background usually works best, since it blends cleanly with different report background colors.

faq.question: Can I make the logo clickable so it links to my company website?
faq.answer: Yes, an inserted logo image supports the same Action settings as other shapes, letting you turn it into a link to a URL or another report page.

faq.question: Will my logo appear the same way on mobile reports?
faq.answer: It's worth checking the mobile layout separately, since logo placement and sizing can need adjustment to look right on a smaller screen.

faq.question: Can I set a logo to appear automatically on every new report?
faq.answer: Power BI doesn't include a built-in default logo template, so many teams handle this by starting new reports from a saved template file that already includes the logo.
